title: Accumulation of change
description: Practice associating the area under rate-of-change curves with net change.

assessment_items.question_content:
It takes $50$ minutes for Joe to notice that his cola bottling machine has sprung a leak. Joe is able to stop the leak in $10$ minutes. The graph below shows the rate at which cola leaks from the machine as a function of time.

![A function is graphed. Time in minutes is on the x-axis. Gallons per minutes is on the y-axis. The graph is a set of line segments. The set starts at (0, 10), moves downward to (50, 8), then moves downward and ends at (60, 0).](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/6caa2cac5775d2c73e3638bbb938cde13e94a491)

**How much cola does Joe lose in this bottling disaster?**

[[☃ input-number 1]] gallons
hints:
The area under the rate curve gives us the amount of cola lost. What is the total area of the two shaded regions below?

![The graph of the function has the area between the line segments and the x-axis shaded and divided into parts. A trapezoidal part labeled T is below the segment moving downward from (0, 10) to (50, 8). A triangular part labeled delta is below the segment moving downward from (50, 8) to (60, 0).](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/80ce81e1f12423d113d40aa06c5e0509e5c4a273)

The areas of the trapezoid and triangle are

$T=\dfrac12 \cdot (10+8)\cdot 50=450$

$\Delta=\dfrac12 \cdot 10\cdot 8=40$.

The amount of cola Joe loses is

$T+\Delta=450+40=490$ gallons.

assessment_items.question_content:
A water cooler springs a leak and empties in $2$ minutes. The graph below shows the rate at which water leaks from the cooler as a function of time.

![A function is graphed. Time in minutes is on the x-axis. Gallons per minutes is on the y-axis. The graph is a line. The line starts at (0, 6), moves downward, and ends at (2, 0).](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/5131235cc2dc126bac57d555cefc7a909b9d7a2a)

**How much water was in the cooler before it started leaking?**

[[☃ input-number 1]] gallons
hints:
The area under the rate curve gives us the amount of water in the cooler before it started leaking. What is the area of the shaded region below?

![The graph of the function has the triangular area between the line and the x-axis shaded.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/56095e3e4798b64973236e948128ac2991bb682d)

The area of the triangle is

$A=\dfrac12 \cdot 2 \cdot 6=6$.
There were $6$ gallons of water in the cooler before it started leaking.

assessment_items.question_content:
A freight train leaves the station on a 4.5-hour trip. The graph below shows the train's velocity as a function of time.

![A function is graphed. Time in hours is on the x-axis. Miles per hour is on the y-axis. The graph is a set of line segments. The set starts at (0, 0), moves upward to (0.5, 40), moves horizontally to the right to (4, 40), moves downward and ends at (4.5, 0).](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/e3b33452db467212f7f6de8cebada7ce54180f2b)

**How far does the train travel?**

[[☃ input-number 1]] miles
hints:
The area under the rate curve gives us the distance the train travels. What is the total area of the three shaded regions below?

![The graph of the function has the area between the line segments and the x-axis shaded and divided into parts. A triangular part labeled delta sub 1 is formed below the segment moving upward from (0, 0) to (0.5, 40). A rectangular part labeled R is below the segment moving horizontally to the right from (0.5, 40) to (3.5, 40). A triangular part labeled delta sub 2 is below the segment moving downward from (3.5, 40) to (4.5, 0).](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/85d74f6ee9fcb48999e0a43f6cb481975a56d7b7)

The areas of the first triangle, rectangle, and second triangle are

$\Delta_1=\dfrac12 \cdot 0.5\cdot 40=10$

$R=3\cdot 40=120$

$\Delta_2=\dfrac12 \cdot 1\cdot 40=20$.

The train travels

$\Delta_1+R+\Delta_2=10+120+20=150$ miles.

assessment_items.question_content:
A freight train leaves the station on a $10$-hour trip. The graph below shows the train's velocity as a function of time.

![A function is graphed. Time in hours is on the x-axis. Miles per hour is on the y-axis. The graph is a set of line segments. The set starts at (0, 0), moves upward to (2, 55), moves horizontally to the right to (9, 55), moves downward and ends at (10, 0).](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/9a2420bc3c9f304c3b2bcc2476dd6cab0241c1fc)

**How far does the train travel?**

[[☃ input-number 1]] miles
hints:
The area under the rate curve gives us the distance the train travels. What is the total area of the three shaded regions below?

![The graph of the function has the area between the line segments and the x-axis shaded and divided into parts. A triangular part labeled delta sub 1 is formed below the segment moving upward from (0, 0) to (2, 55). A rectangular part labeled R is below the segment moving horizontally to the right from (2, 55) to (9, 55). A triangular part labeled delta sub 2 is below the segment moving downward from (9, 55) to (10, 0).](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/c8598299e8c4a4fce3bedb3ec6668c11f088b43f)

The areas of the first triangle, rectangle, and second triangle are

$\Delta_1=\dfrac12 \cdot 2\cdot 55=55$

$R=7\cdot 55=385$

$\Delta_2=\dfrac12 \cdot 1\cdot 55=27.5$.

The train travels

$\Delta_1+R+\Delta_2=55+385+27.5=467.5$ miles.

assessment_items.question_content:
A freight train leaves the station on an $8$-hour trip. The graph below shows the train's velocity as a function of time.

![A function is graphed. Time in hours is on the x-axis. Miles per hour is on the y-axis. The graph is a set of line segments. The set starts at (0, 0), moves upward to (1, 50), moves horizontally to the right to (7, 50), moves downward and ends at (8, 0).](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/bbd341363bce65daf82a9b191090ff3c3729100d)

**How far does the train travel?**

[[☃ input-number 1]] miles
hints:
The area under the rate curve gives us the distance the train travels. What is the total area of the three shaded regions below?

![The graph of the function has the area between the line segments and the x-axis shaded and divided into parts. A triangular part labeled delta sub 1 is formed below the segment moving upward from (0, 0) to (1, 50). A rectangular part labeled R is below the segment moving horizontally to the right from (1, 50) to (7, 50). A triangular part labeled delta sub 2 is below the segment moving downward from (7, 50) and (8, 0).](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/54c5216c0121e1a7aa34653c793d0da05fc9eeb5)

The areas of the first triangle, rectangle, and second triangle are

$\Delta_1=\dfrac12 \cdot 1\cdot 50=25$

$R=6\cdot 50=300$

$\Delta_2=\dfrac12 \cdot 1\cdot 50=25$.

The train travels

$\Delta_1+R+\Delta_2=25+300+25=350$ miles.

assessment_items.question_content:
A water tank holds $375$ gallons. A drain is opened to let water out of the tank. The drain is closed $60$ minutes later. The graph below shows the rate at which water drains from the tank as a function of time.

![A function is graphed. Time in minutes is on the x-axis. Gallons per minute is on the y-axis. The graph is a line. The line starts at (0, 10), moves downward, and ends at (60, 2).](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/23a2dcefbf73cf98a1e461f2102e142b52e53234)

**How much water *remains* in the tank after the drain is closed?**

[[☃ input-number 1]] gallons
hints:
The area under the rate curve gives us the amount of water drained from the tank. What is the area of the shaded region below?

![The graph of the function has the trapezoidal area between the line and the x-axis shaded.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/55b398e954f33dc777ef0652d45b894281367c25)

The area of the trapezoid is

$A=\dfrac12 \cdot (10+2)\cdot 60=360$.

How much water remains in the $375$ gallon tank after draining $360$ gallons?

The amount of water remaining in the tank is

$375-360=15$ gallons.

assessment_items.question_content:
A freight train leaves the station on a $10$-hour trip. The graph below shows the train's velocity as a function of time.

![A function is graphed. Time in hours is on the x-axis. Miles per hour is on the y-axis. The graph is a set of line segments. The set starts at (0, 0), moves upward to (1.5, 60), moves horizontally to the right to (8.5, 60), moves downward and ends at (10, 0).](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/eae0280c6de9301bc780e552e5eef1e9087f12f6)

**How far does the train travel?**

[[☃ input-number 1]] miles
hints:
The area under the rate curve gives us the distance the train travels. What is the total area of the three shaded regions below?

![The graph of the function has the area between the line segments and the x-axis shaded and divided into parts. A triangular part labeled delta sub 1 is formed below the segment moving upward from (0, 0) to (1.5, 60). A rectangular part labeled R is below the segment moving horizontally to the right from (1.5, 60) to (8.5, 60). A triangular part labeled delta sub 2 is below the segment moving downward from (8.5, 60) to (10, 0).](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/79c0ecafa01a766f374fbfd87bb96f43b535ed57)

The areas of the first triangle, rectangle, and second triangle are

$\Delta_1=\dfrac12 \cdot 1.5 \cdot 60=45$

$R=7\cdot 60=420$

$\Delta_2=\dfrac12 \cdot 1.5 \cdot 60=45$.

The train travels

$\Delta_1+R+\Delta_2=45+420+45=510$ miles.

assessment_items.question_content:
The natatorium opens $2$ hours before the start of a swim meet. The graph below shows the rate at which fans enter the natatorium as a function of time.

![A function is graphed. Time in hours is on the x-axis. Fans per hour is on the y-axis. The graph is a set of line segments. The set starts at (0, 400), moves upward to (1, 800), moves horizontally to the right, and ends at (2, 800).](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/af061ec6a2b6288edb7a24590d889f64ccc2266a)

**How many fans attend the swim meet?**

[[☃ input-number 1]]  fans
hints:
The area under the rate curve gives us the number of fans who attend the event. What is the total area of the two shaded regions below?

![The graph of the function has the area between the line segments and the x-axis shaded and divided into parts. A trapezoidal part labeled T is below the segment moving upward from (0, 400) to (1, 800). A rectangular part labeled R is below the segment moving horizontally to the right from (1, 800) to (2, 800).](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/88302430f9ab5b99361ed4b5eee5eefcd02ef905)

The areas of the trapezoid and rectangle are

$T=\dfrac12 (400+800)\cdot1=600$

$R=1\cdot800=800$.

The event is attended by

$T+R=600+800=1400$ fans.

assessment_items.question_content:
A freight train leaves the station on a $10$-hour trip. The graph below shows the train's velocity as a function of time.

![A function is graphed. Time in hours is on the x-axis. Miles per hour is on the y-axis. The graph is a set of line segments. The set starts at (0, 0), moves upward to (1, 40), moves horizontally to the right to (3, 40), moves upward to (4, 50), moves horizontally to the right to (9, 50), moves downward and ends at (10, 0).](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/78f06301fd3b348e2abe0a1bb4300e6313cde06b)

**How far does the train travel?**

[[☃ input-number 1]] miles
hints:
The area under the rate curve gives us the distance the train travels. What is the total area of the five shaded regions below?

![The graph of the function has the area between the line segments and the x-axis shaded and divided into parts. A triangular part labeled delta sub 1 is below the segment moving upward from (0, 0) to (1, 40). A rectangular part labeled R sub 1 is below the horizontal segment moving rightward from (1, 40) to (3, 40). A trapezoidal part labeled T is below the segment moving upward from (3, 40) to (4, 50). A rectangular part labeled R sub 2 is below the horizontal segment moving rightward from (4, 50) to (9, 50). A triangular part labeled delta sub 2 is below the segment moving downward from (9, 50) to (10, 0).](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/534f68b3d8e7c2c44dcbad4dd60a1bc1141cdbc0)

The areas of the first triangle, first rectangle, trapezoid, second rectangle, and second triangle are

$\Delta_1=\dfrac12 \cdot 1\cdot 40=20$

$R_1=2\cdot 40=80$

$T=\dfrac12\cdot (40+50)\cdot 1=45$

$R_2=5\cdot 50=250$

$\Delta_2=\dfrac12 \cdot 1\cdot 50=25$.

The train travels

$\Delta_1+R_1+T+R_2+\Delta_2=20+80+45+250+25=420$ miles.

assessment_items.question_content:
A snowstorm lasts $18$ hours. The graph below shows the rate of snowfall as a function of time.

![A function is graphed. Time in hours is on the x-axis. Inches per hour is on the y-axis. The graph is a set of line segments. The set starts at (0, 0), moves upward to (2, 1), moves horizontally to the right to (12, 1), moves downward to (14, 0.5), moves horizontally to the right to (16, 0.5), moves downward and ends at (18, 0).](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/5f5686f6dbe4d64ed8932734b864c9484fda4c57)

**What is the total snowfall?**

[[☃ input-number 1]] inches
hints:
The area under the rate curve gives us the amount of snowfall. What is the total area of the five shaded regions below?

![The graph of the function has the area between the line segments and the x-axis shaded and divided into parts. A triangular part labeled delta sub 1 is below the segment moving upward from (0, 0) to (2, 1). A rectangular part labeled R sub 1 is below the horizontal segment moving rightward from (2, 1) to (12, 1). A trapezoidal part labeled T is below the segment moving downward from (12, 1) to (14, 0.5). A rectangular part labeled R sub 2 is below the horizontal segment moving rightward from (14, 0.5) to (16, 0.5). A triangular part labeled delta sub 2 is below the segment moving downward from (16, 0.5) to (18, 0).](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/59be7eb71866eba70b3c3d0d1050ec012efe7cc8)

The areas of the first triangle, first rectangle, trapezoid, second rectangle, and second triangle are

$\Delta_1=\dfrac12 \cdot 2\cdot 1=1$

$R_1=10\cdot 1=10$

$T=\dfrac12 (1+0.5)\cdot2=1.5$

$R_2=2\cdot 0.5=1$

$\Delta_2=\dfrac12 \cdot 2\cdot 0.5=0.5$.

The total snowfall is

$\Delta_1+R_1+T+R_2+\Delta_2=1+10+1.5+1+0.5=14$ inches.

assessment_items.question_content:
A snowstorm lasts $24$ hours. The graph below shows the rate of snowfall as a function of time.

![A function is graphed. Time in hours is on the x-axis. Inches per hour is on the y-axis. The graph is a set of line segments. The set starts at (0, 0), moves upward to (4, 0.5), moves horizontally to the right to (8, 0.5), moves upward to (12, 1), moves horizontally to the right to (20, 1), moves downward and ends at (24, 0).](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/6c848339c299b4f45e059aa5c1cce57a8a755adc)

**What is the total snowfall?**

[[☃ input-number 1]] inches
hints:
The area under the rate curve gives us the amount of snowfall. What is the total area of the five shaded regions below?

![The graph of the function has the area between the line segments and the x-axis shaded and divided into parts. A triangular part labeled delta sub 1 is below the segment moving upward from (0, 0) to (4, 0.5). A rectangular part labeled R sub 1 is below the horizontal segment moving rightward from (4, 0.5) to (8, 0.5). A trapezoidal part labeled T is below the segment moving upward from (8, 0.5) to (12, 1). A rectangular part labeled R sub 2 is below the horizontal segment moving rightward from (12, 1) to (20, 1). A triangular part labeled delta sub 2 is below the segment moving downward from (20, 1) to (24, 0).](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/2d468fa5f3f38634201013e53e656d7a08a27fcd)

The areas of the first triangle, first rectangle, trapezoid, second rectangle, and second triangle are

$\Delta_1=\dfrac12 \cdot 4\cdot 0.5=1$

$R_1=4\cdot 0.5=2$

$T=\dfrac12 (0.5+1)\cdot4=3$

$R_2=8\cdot1=8$

$\Delta_2=\dfrac12 \cdot 4\cdot 1=2$.

The total snowfall is

$\Delta_1+R_1+T+R_2+\Delta_2=1+2+3+8+2=16$ inches.

assessment_items.question_content:
Firemen spray water on a house fire. The spray rate steadily increases until it reaches its maximum capacity, $24$ gallons per minute. It takes $100$ minutes to put out the fire. The graph below shows the spray rate as a function of time.

![A function is graphed. Time in minutes is on the x-axis. Gallons per minutes is on the y-axis. The graph is a set of line segments. The set starts at (0, 0), moves upward to (20, 24), moves horizontally to the right, and ends at (100, 24).](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/cd228b9f5587d0cc410499efb1ab05cdfc32ac95)

**How much water do the firemen use to put out the fire?**

[[☃ input-number 1]] gallons
hints:
The area under the rate curve gives us the amount of water used. What is the total area of the two shaded regions below?

![The graph of the function has the area between the line segments and the x-axis shaded and divided into parts. A triangular part labeled R sub 1 is below the segment moving upward from (0, 0) to (20, 24). A rectangular part labeled R sub 2 is below the segment moving horizontally to the right from (20, 24) to (100, 24).](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/2b4b3bfa923bd587e2f82e22411b7d13175b97a9)

The areas of the triangle and rectangle are

$R_1=\dfrac{20\cdot 24}{2}=240$

$R_2=80\cdot 24=1920$

The total amount of water used to put out the fire is

$R_1+R_2=240+1920=2160$ gallons.
